name: Kefir (food medium)
basis: per 100 g whole-milk kefir (dairy, acid-alcoholic fermented cow milk)

components.name: water
components.source: USDA FDC 170904 (kefir, lowfat, plain, LIFEWAY); milk matrix
components.evidence: Kefir is ~88-90 g water/100 g; solvent of the medium.

components.name: lactose
components.evidence: Residual lactose after ~20-30% is hydrolysed by beta-galactosidase during fermentation; still the largest single sugar.

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: USDA FDC 170904 (total sugars 4.61 minus lactose 3.70) + Frontiers Microbiol 2015 6:1177 (lactose hydrolysis)
components.evidence: Liberated by lactose hydrolysis; preferentially consumed by LAB so it stays low. Approximate split of the ~0.9 g free-monosaccharide pool.

components.name: D-galactose
components.source: USDA FDC 170904 total-minus-lactose pool + Frontiers Microbiol 2015 6:1177
components.evidence: Released 1:1 with glucose from lactose but poorly fermented by most kefir LAB, so galactose accumulates above glucose. Approximate.

components.name: L-lactic acid
components.source: Guzel-Seydim et al. 2000 J Dairy Sci 83:275 (PMID 10714860; up to 7739 ppm = 0.77 g/100g by d21)
components.evidence: DOMINANT fermentation metabolite and a major carbon/energy source; the single most important item that was missing. Titratable acidity of kefir ~0.6-1.0% as lactic.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Guzel-Seydim et al. 2000 J Dairy Sci 83:275 (PMID 10714860); milk citrate ~0.16 g/100g
components.evidence: Milk-derived citrate, partly consumed by citrate-fermenting LAB. Real carbon source.

components.name: acetic acid
components.source: Frontiers Microbiol 2015 6:1177; kefir reviews (acetic-acid bacteria + heterofermentation)
components.evidence: Low but characteristic acetic acid from acetic-acid bacteria/heterofermentative LAB; trace-level, variable.

components.name: pyruvic acid
components.source: Guzel-Seydim et al. 2000 (PMID 10714860; pyruvic produced during fermentation)
components.evidence: Transient trace produced during fermentation; near limit of detection.

components.name: orotic acid
components.source: Guzel-Seydim et al. 2000 (PMID 10714860); milk orotic acid
components.evidence: Milk-derived orotate, slightly increases during storage.

components.name: ethanol
components.evidence: Defining acid-alcoholic co-product from kefir yeasts; modern/commercial kefir ~0.05-0.5%, homemade higher. Value is a typical modern midpoint (range 0.02-1.0).

components.name: carbon dioxide
components.evidence: Dissolved CO2 gives kefir its light carbonation; present but not routinely quantified in g/100g.

components.name: total protein
components.source: USDA FDC 170904 (3.79 g, lowfat) / whole-milk kefir ~3.3 g
components.evidence: Casein + whey; partially hydrolysed during fermentation raising free amino acids/peptides.

components.name: L-tryptophan
components.evidence: Milk-protein-derived; free fraction rises with kefir proteolysis.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Milk-protein-derived (Glu + Gln); most abundant AA, key N source.

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: Milk-protein-derived. (FooDB 'D-Serine' relabelled to the physiological L form.)

components.name: total fat
components.source: whole-milk kefir; USDA whole milk FDC 746782 (lowfat kefir ~1.0 g, FDC 170904)
components.evidence: Milk triacylglycerol; drops to ~1 g/100g in lowfat kefir.

components.name: palmitic acid (16:0)
components.evidence: Dominant saturated milk fatty acid (~30% of fat).

components.name: oleic acid (18:1 n-9)
components.source: USDA whole milk 3.25% (mono total 0.69, mostly oleic)

components.name: myristic acid (14:0)
components.evidence: Major medium-chain saturated milk fatty acid.

components.name: myristoleic acid (14:1)
components.evidence: Minor monounsaturated milk fatty acid; genuine (moved from uncovered).

components.name: cholesterol
components.evidence: GENUINE in dairy (not noise here - kefir is an animal product); scales with fat, ~4 mg/100g in lowfat kefir.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Milk is iron-poor; single iron entry (FooDB triplicate Fe2+/Fe3+/mitochondrial collapsed).

components.name: iodine
components.evidence: Milk trace mineral; variable with feed.

components.name: riboflavin (B2)
components.evidence: Notable milk/kefir vitamin; can rise with fermentation.

components.name: folate (B9)
components.evidence: Milk B vitamin; kefir folate can increase during fermentation.

components.name: vitamin B12 (cyanocobalamin)
components.evidence: Milk-derived cobalamin; key vitamin for many auxotrophs.

components.name: vitamin A (retinol)
components.evidence: Fat-soluble; fortified lowfat kefir products report much higher (up to ~170 ug RAE) due to added retinyl palmitate.

components.name: ammonium
components.evidence: Non-protein nitrogen present; released by microbial deamination during fermentation. Not routinely quantified.

remove.name: Sucrose C12H22O11
remove.reason: Plain kefir contains no sucrose; only sweetened/flavored kefir does. FooDB carry-over, not a component of the plain fermented-milk medium.

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: Plant furanocoumarin (celery/citrus); biologically implausible in a cow-milk product. FooDB predicted noise.

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: Microbial preservative/food additive, not an intrinsic kefir nutrient. FooDB carry-over.

remove.name: (E)-2-Phenyl-2-butenal
remove.reason: Trace aroma volatile, not a growth nutrient; FooDB flavor-compound listing.

remove.name: (+/-)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: Predicted non-physiological stereoisomer; the real component is L-isoleucine (retained).

remove.name: 24,25-Dihydroxyvitamin D3
remove.reason: Predicted vitamin D catabolite at ultratrace level; not a meaningful growth nutrient.

remove.name: Co2+ (free cobalt ion)
remove.reason: Ultratrace/predicted; biologically relevant cobalt is bound in vitamin B12, which is retained. Free ionic cobalt is FooDB noise.

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Ultratrace element, not a defined milk growth nutrient; FooDB trace-element noise.

remove.name: Chromium
remove.reason: Ultratrace element; not a growth-defining nutrient of kefir.

remove.name: Nonanoate (C9:0)
remove.reason: Odd-chain C9 fatty acid essentially absent from bovine milk fat; FooDB artifact (milk FAs are even-chain 4:0-18:x).

remove.name: Molybdate
remove.reason: Ultratrace; not a quantified growth-relevant milk nutrient. Dropped as noise (kept out of mineral panel).

missing_added: L-lactic acid (DOMINANT fermentation metabolite, ~0.8 g/100g - the single biggest omission)
D-glucose and D-galactose (from lactose hydrolysis; galactose accumulates)
quantitative amounts for all previously name-only amino acids, fatty acids, minerals and vitamins
keep_uncovered: beta-Lactose (anomer of lactose - already captured quantitatively as lactose)
myristoleic acid (genuine minor milk fatty acid - added to components)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 170904 (Kefir, lowfat, plain, LIFEWAY) - sugars, protein, minerals, water-soluble vitamins
USDA FoodData Central FDC 172217 / 746782 (Milk, whole 3.25%) - fatty acid profile, cholesterol, fat-soluble vitamins
USDA whole cow milk amino-acid profile (SR) - amino acids
Guzel-Seydim et al. 2000, J Dairy Sci 83(2):275 (PMID 10714860) - organic acids (lactic up to 7739 ppm, citric, pyruvic, orotic)
Prado et al. 2015, Front Microbiol 6:1177 (Milk kefir: composition, microbial cultures) - lactose hydrolysis, ethanol, acetic acid, CO2
McCance & Widdowson (UK) - chloride, iodine, biotin milk values
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources are lactose (~3.7 g) plus its hydrolysis products glucose+galactose (~0.9 g) AND lactic acid (~0.8 g) - the last was entirely missing from the FooDB profile and is the defining metabolite of this acid-alcoholic fermented milk. Ethanol, acetic acid, citric and orotic acids are the other key organic-acid/co-product additions. Basis is whole-milk kefir; lowfat kefir has ~1 g fat (fatty acids and cholesterol scale down ~3x, other values similar). Cholesterol was RETAINED (kefir is a dairy/animal product, so it is genuine, not plant-food noise).
